evidence.title: Notamify January 2026 Tehran FIR closure comparison
evidence.source_description: Notamify page for OIIX A0225/26, the January 2026 Tehran FIR closure matching the market packet's qualifying example, captured by web.open during defendant merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Provides a same-database contrast between a true Tehran FIR airspace closure (A0225/26, QAFLC, Airspace Restrictions, Tehran FIR closed to all flights except approved international civil arrivals/departures) and plaintiff's A1010/26 airport-operations NOTAM (QFALC).

evidence.title: SafeAirspace Iran OIIX western FIR context
evidence.source_description: SafeAirspace Iran page captured via web.open/find during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Aviation-risk context showing western Tehran FIR remained closed in June 2026 after the broader Iran airspace crisis, supporting the significance of western OIIX restrictions.

evidence.title: Arab Times May 23 2026 CAA NOTAM report
evidence.source_description: Arab Times article captured via web.open during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Independent corroborating report that Iran's CAA issued a NOTAM closing all airports in western Tehran FIR except limited daylight operations and suspending prior permissions.

evidence.title: Indian Express May 23 2026 Iran western airspace report
evidence.source_description: Indian Express article captured via web.open during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Corroborates that Iran's Civil Aviation Authority issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 closing western Tehran FIR airports from May 22 to May 25 and requiring fresh approvals.

evidence.title: Notamify OIIX A1010/26 raw NOTAM capture
evidence.source_description: Notamify page for Tehran FIR/OIIX NOTAM A1010/26, captured via web.open during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Contains the raw NOTAM text and interpretation for OIIX A1010/26, the core source for the alleged Iran-initiated closure effective May 22-25, 2026.

evidence.title: FAA international NOTAM Q-code decode: FA/AF and LC
evidence.source_description: FAA Order 7930.2 Appendix B, International NOTAM (Q) Codes, captured by web.open/find during defendant merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Explains the NOTAM Q-code structure and decodes the raw NOTAM codes at issue: FA means aerodrome, AF means flight information region, and LC means closed. Supports distinguishing A1010/26 (QFALC, aerodrome closed) from an FIR airspace closure code (QAFLC).
